risk_rationale: The regulatory signal from Australia indicates a potential shift towards mandatory age verification measures, which could impact TikTok's current age-gated account controls. This presents a moderate risk as it may require adjustments to existing systems to comply with new verification standards.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Minimum registration age is 13 years globally, with regional variations.
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Explores age verification measures to protect children from online harms.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The roadmap suggests implementing age verification measures, which may require changes to current age-gating processes.

remediations.title: Implement Age Verification System
remediations.description: Develop and integrate an age verification system that complies with the new regulatory guidance from the Australian eSafety Commissioner.
acceptance_criteria: Age verification system is implemented and operational in Australia.
System complies with the eSafety Commissioner's guidelines.

business_impact: The introduction of age verification measures may increase user onboarding friction, potentially affecting user growth metrics in Australia.
recommended_actions: Conduct a feasibility study on age verification technologies.
Engage with the eSafety Commissioner to understand detailed requirements.
Prepare a compliance roadmap for potential implementation.
